HRVDex_2026-06-25_equiv.node-export.json.note: REGENERATED 2026-06-25 (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-FOLLOWUPS-VII §1) — the recording block was re-sorted. hrvBuildNodeExport now sorts rows ascending by floating tMs (ONE ordering rule for both the app's exportGanglior and the headless HRVDex.compute({text}) path); previously the headless path left the newest-first Welltory rows in file order, so recording.startEpochMs/firstTMs was the LAST day, lastTMs the earliest, and spanDays NEGATIVE (-27) — a Clock-Contract §4/§6 violation (startEpochMs must be the EARLIEST valid sample). Re-run HRVDex.compute({text}) on the committed Welltory CSV (uploads/WELLTORY_HRV_DATA_EXPORT_20_May_2026_12_00_AM-17_Jun_2026_11_59_PM.csv); the equivalence-gate diff (excludes generated/provenance/kernel/vo2est/karv) reproduces it with 0 differing fields. 0 events (no rmssd<20 / stress>=70 row on this real file) — event byte-coverage is carried by the new HRVDex_2026-06-25_events fixture below. HRVDex_2026-06-25_events.node-export.jso.note: ADDED 2026-06-25 (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-FOLLOWUPS-VII §2) — event-byte-coverage for the equivalence gate (env.equiv.hrvdex_events). The -VI HRVDex equiv fixture carried ZERO events, so the event t-string/conf/meta/ordering went untested. uploads/HRVDex_2026-06-25_events.csv is a small purpose-built Welltory-format summary CSV (4 rows, multi-day, newest-first to also exercise the VII §1 sort) crafted with rows that cross both thresholds: rMSSD<20 → hrv_low (measured) and Stress(HRV)>=70 → stress_high (meta.derived:true, heuristic tier). Produced by running the current HRVDex bundle (manifestHash 12ee06f41b0f) headless via HRVDex.compute({text}) → 4 events (hrv_low+stress_high at the oldest row, hrv_low, stress_high), sorted ascending by tMs, spanDays 4. This IS hrvBuildNodeExport's output (the app delegates to it) → byte-identical to HRVDex.html's own export. Fully regenerate-able: re-run HRVDex.compute({text}) on the committed CSV; the gate diff (excludes generated/kernel) reproduces it with 0 differing fields. GATE B asserts the producing bundle's CURRENT manifestHash still equals 12ee06f41b0f. NO extra re-bundle (rides VII §1's HRVDex bundle).

synthetic_hrvdex_golden.node-export.json.note: ADDED 2026-07-12 (CPAP-REAL-CORPUS-2026-07-11-BRIEF §P9, resolving §M5) — a COMMITTED, content-addressed equivalence input for HRVDex. Before P9, every node's equivalence input was a REAL recording, hence gitignored, hence absent on a fresh clone: ALL EIGHT compute()-equivalence legs SKIPPED in CI. The suite's strongest correctness gate — the GATE-C surface that catches a code change which moved a fixture's OUTPUT — therefore ran only on the maintainer's machine, and a regression that moved an export would have gone green through CI. This input is SYNTHETIC (tools/make-synthetic-inputs.mjs): the vendor FORMAT is reproduced exactly, the DATA never is — closed-form, physiologically plausible waveforms containing no recording of any person and no device identifier. Deterministic (no RNG, no Date.now()), so re-running the generator reproduces it byte-for-byte and inputHashes stay stable. It ADDS to the real-recording leg (which still runs locally and exercises genuine vendor quirks); it does not replace it. REGENERATE the input and this golden together whenever the export shape changes.
